title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Access Points, and Tenant Isolation · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs iam, bucket policies, access points, and tenant isolation from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Policy Simulation Negativ…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1884 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

Inspect the Policy Simulation Negative Tests trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Policy Simulation Negativ….
